author: Claus-Peter Käpplinger
message:
Add --crawl-ignore-ssl flag to disable crawler SSL verification (#77)

Opt-in flag for the async DocCrawler that disables TLS certificate
verification via aiohttp.TCPConnector(ssl=False), for crawling sites with
self-signed or otherwise invalid certificates. Off by default; prints a
runtime warning when enabled.

Squashed from PR #77 and resolved the _init_session conflict to keep the
existing aiohttp ClientTimeout alongside the new optional connector.

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Fix web app mislabeling successful repos as errors + oversized error icon

Two Conduit rendering bugs, both triggered by processing a repo that contains
XML-like tag text in its files — e.g. onefilellm's own repo (the most obvious
thing to test):

- classify() mislabeled a SUCCESSFUL fetch as a processing error. It stripped
  <file>/<page> bodies and scanned the remainder for <error>, but file CONTENT
  routinely contains literal </file> and <error...> strings (onefilellm's source
  builds those tags), which broke the non-greedy strip and matched a regex
  fragment from file content — surfacing "]*>(.*?)" as the error message on a
  141k-token result. Now classify by the PRESENCE of <file>/<page> children: a
  fetch that produced structured content is a success; a root-level <error> is a
  hard failure only when there are no such children. Add a regression test.

- The error-state icon had no width/height, so the inline <svg> ballooned to fill
  the card and crushed the message into a one-character-per-line column. Constrain
  .error__icon to 1em (1.5rem).

80 web-app tests pass; the real jimmc414/onefilellm repo now classifies as
success (141,363 tokens) instead of showing a broken error card.

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Fix SSRF protection bypass in web app URL guard (#79)

The web app's _is_safe_url() validated only urlparse(url).hostname, which an
attacker can make disagree with the host requests/urllib3 actually connects to.
Reported payload: http://127.0.0.1:6666\@arxiv.org — urlparse reads the host as
"arxiv.org" while requests normalises the backslash and connects to 127.0.0.1.

An adversarial review of the hardened guard surfaced two further bypass classes
and one over-block, all now fixed and regression-tested:

- Parser / percent-encoding differential (the #79 class): validate the EXACT
  host requests will dial, via requests.PreparedRequest.prepare_url (which
  percent-decodes and IDNA-encodes), instead of urlparse's raw host. Closes the
  backslash and user@host forms, the percent-encoded variants
  (http://127.0.0.%31/, http://169.254.169.%32%35%34/ → cloud metadata), and the
  legacy decimal/octal/hex IPv4 notations (2130706433, 0177.0.0.1, 0x7f000001).
- Public name that resolves to a private IP: resolve the host and reject if any
  address is private/loopback/link-local/reserved/multicast/unspecified — catches
  *.nip.io, localtest.me, ip6-localhost. Fails open on resolution error so
  offline/air-gapped use still works; IP literals are blocked without any lookup.
- IDN over-block: validating the prepared (punycode) host means internationalised
  domains (münchen.de, россия.рф) are no longer wrongly rejected.

The guard still rejects backslashes / control chars / whitespace and embedded
credentials before any fetch, and runs on both POST / and GET /stream ahead of
every network call.

Out of scope (documented in the guard): DNS rebinding — a hostile resolver
returning a public address at validation and a private one at fetch time — would
require pinning the connection to the validated IP in the HTTP layer.

Add SSRF regression coverage to tests/test_web_app.py: a verified 39-URL
must-block corpus (incl. the #79 payload and the percent-encoded/resolver
vectors) and an 18-URL must-allow corpus, with DNS monkeypatched so the suite
stays hermetic. 79 web-app tests pass.

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Redesign extras/web_app.py as "Conduit" with live SSE progress

Replace the blocking, single-<pre> Flask web app with a single-column
command console (terminal x SaaS) that streams progress and tames large
output, without weakening the security model. The whole front end is
offline-safe (no CDN, no web fonts, no icon fonts, no build step; vanilla
JS, system fonts, inline SVG) and degrades to a working no-JS fallback.

Backend (extras/web_app.py):
- Add GET /stream Server-Sent Events: a daemon worker thread runs the
  pipeline and feeds a bounded queue that the Flask generator drains;
  emits stage/page/done/error events with : connected, 15s keepalives,
  and a None sentinel; sets text/event-stream headers with no
  Content-Length and no compression.
- Single-flight threading.Lock (a second concurrent job gets error(busy));
  client disconnect sets a cancel Event that cooperatively stops the crawl
  and (once set) switches all queue puts to drop-on-full so the worker can
  never wedge the lock; the whole worker is wrapped so any exception
  becomes a terminal error(internal) and the lock is always released.
- Move clipboard copy client-side: add GET /result/raw (text/plain inline)
  reusing the two-file download whitelist; remove the server-side pyperclip
  copy and the dead processed_urls.txt write.
- Add a robust result classifier (token-count empty backstop, then strip
  <page>/<file> subtrees before matching a source-level <error>, so a
  partially failed crawl still counts as success), metrics (estimated model
  tokens = round(uncompressed * 1.37)), a 256 KB char-boundary-bounded
  preview, and a server-parsed file/page node list. Keep _is_safe_url and
  /download byte-for-byte; run with threaded=True.
- Retain POST / as a synchronous no-JS fallback that renders the same
  bounded result/error server-side.

Core (onefilellm.py):
- Add a backward-compatible progress_callback=None to crawl_and_extract_text
  (guarded so a faulty callback can never break the crawl; returning False
  cooperatively stops between pages). All existing callers and tests are
  unaffected.

Front end (extras/templates/index.html, extras/static/):
- New app shell with empty-state teaching gallery, live input-type
  detection badge, stage log, determinate/indeterminate progress, metrics
  panel, virtualized overview tree, bounded raw preview, client-side copy,
  downloads, toasts, optional session history, light/dark themes, and full
  keyboard/ARIA support honoring prefers-reduced-motion.

Docs & tests:
- Add tests/test_web_app.py (Flask test_client, backend monkeypatched, no
  network): endpoint behavior, the classifier, the /result/raw + /download
  whitelist, and the security blocks. Save the approved design spec and the
  upstream brief; update extras/readme.md and extras/requirements.txt
  (Flask only).

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Add changelog and update docs for new YouTube transcript flow

- Add CHANGELOG.md covering all 294 commits (2023-03 through 2026-06),
  grouped by month with commit/PR links, generated from git history and
  spot-verified against GitHub PRs
- Update docs/architecture.md call tree to the current yt-dlp ->
  subtitle-discovery -> transcript-api-fallback flow and add yt-dlp to
  the dependency list
- Expand README troubleshooting: explain the two-fetcher design, advise
  upgrading yt-dlp and youtube-transcript-api when fetching breaks, and
  recommend Node.js for reliable yt-dlp extraction

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Fix YouTube transcript fetching broken by yt-dlp and transcript-api changes

yt-dlp path:
- Use --write-auto-subs/--write-subs with --sub-langs "en,en-orig" and
  --convert-subs srt; broad patterns like "en.*" expand to translation
  codes (en-de, ...) and trigger HTTP 429
- Add --js-runtimes node so extraction uses the full client instead of
  the degraded android_vr fallback that intermittently yields zero
  subtitle formats; retry without the flag on older yt-dlp releases
- Replace hardcoded {id}.en.vtt lookup with glob-based discovery that
  prefers English variants, surviving lang-code drift (en-orig, en-US)
- Scan for subtitle files regardless of exit code: a 429 on a secondary
  language returns nonzero even when the primary file was written
- Skip Kind:/Language: header lines and collapse consecutive duplicate
  lines from rolling auto-captions (previously ~3x duplicated output)

youtube_transcript_api fallback:
- Migrate off static get_transcript(), deprecated in 1.0 and removed in
  1.2, to the v1.x instance API (.fetch with .list fallback for
  non-English videos); keep legacy path for <1.0
- Bump pin from 0.4.1 (broken against current YouTube backend) to
  >=1.1.0,<2 in requirements.txt and pyproject.toml
- Report both failures when yt-dlp and the fallback both fail instead
  of silently dropping the fallback exception

author: Jim McMillan
message:
Fix 39 defects from comprehensive security and code quality audit

CRITICAL:
- Web app: fix path traversal in /download, block local path processing,
  disable debug mode, bind to localhost only (C1-C3)
- Fix unreachable duplicate except block in _fetch_url_content (C4)
- Use aiohttp.ClientTimeout instead of raw int for crawler timeouts (C5)
- Add asyncio.Lock to protect shared state in DocCrawler workers (C6)
- Fix try/finally scoping so args is always defined after parsing (C7)

HIGH:
- Only send GitHub token to github.com domains, not arbitrary URLs (H1)
- Wrap crawl regex patterns in try/except re.error for ReDoS safety (H2)
- Cap issue fetching at 500 to prevent memory exhaustion (H3)
- Add pagination to all comment-fetching endpoints (H4)
- Sync yt-dlp/youtube-transcript-api across requirements.txt and pyproject.toml (H5)
- Fix mutable default argument in get_token_count (H6)
- Fix alias listing to show core aliases even when overridden (H7)
- Use apparent_encoding to avoid ISO-8859-1 default for UTF-8 pages (H8)

MEDIUM/LOW:
- Strip query params from ArXiv URLs before PDF construction (M5)
- Fix off-by-one in is_within_depth by filtering empty path segments (M9)
- Add 50MB response size limit to async crawler (M10)
- Use deque + set for O(1) URL dedup in sync crawler (M11)
- Propagate download_file errors instead of silently swallowing (M12)
- Replace bare except: with except Exception: (L2)
- Remove redundant regex anchors with fullmatch (L8)
